titleStmt.title: Letter from Frank Shawe-Taylor to Matthew Nathan, 18 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Frank M. Shawe-Taylor

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Frank M. Shawe-Taylor to Sir Matthew Nathan acknowledging receipt of the second moiety of the allowance he paid to his under sheriff. Enclosed is the receipt for the money reimbursed to Shawe-Taylor.Frank Shawe-Taylor (1869-1920) was a land agent and high sheriff for County Galway. Shawe-Taylor was killed in an IRA ambush in 1920. It is said that Shawe-Taylor's death had a violent aftermath due to reprisal killings on both sides.Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) served as the under secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5596/164/17

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
MOOR PARK, ATHENRY, CO. GALWAY. Dec. 18. 1915 Dear Sir, In reply to your circular of the
16th inst. I herewith enclose
a voucher for the 2nd moiety
of Under Sheriff Allowances
as requested. Yrs faithfully Frank. M. Shawe. Taylor.
High Sheriff Co Galway The Under Secretary Dublin Castle I Ack. of request the High Sheriff to furnish
the under Sheriff receipt
II. J.D.
LJT.
20.12.1915 I Done 20/12
Co. Galway 17th Decr 1915. Received from Under Secretary Dublin Castle
the sum of
sixty two Pounds ten
Shillings, and Pence
being second moiety of allowance for Under-Sheriff for 1915.
ÃÂ£62=10=0 Frank. M. Shawe. Taylor.
